race.name: Wasatch Citizens Series
race.tagline: Twenty-five kilometers on Olympic-legacy trails — citizen racing on the 2002 Winter Games cross-country venue in Utah's Wasatch Range.

climate.description: Utah's Wasatch Range receives legendary dry powder snow. Temperatures typically -15°C to -2°C. High-altitude dry conditions provide excellent grooming.
challenges: Utah's dry air requires hydration attention
Occasional inversion layers in the valley
Sun exposure on open course sections

course.primary: 25km course on the Soldier Hollow Nordic center, venue of the 2002 Salt Lake City Winter Olympics cross-country and biathlon events, in the Wasatch Range.

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Olympic legacy venue with excellent grooming and infrastructure. Utah's dry powder provides superb conditions.

history.summary: The Wasatch Citizens Series takes advantage of the Olympic-legacy infrastructure at Soldier Hollow, built for the 2002 Salt Lake City Winter Olympics. The venue continues to host national-level competitions alongside citizen races.
notable_facts: Soldier Hollow hosted the 2002 Olympic cross-country and biathlon events
Olympic-standard trail grooming and infrastructure remain
Utah is bidding to host another Winter Olympics (2034)
Wasatch Range receives an average of 12+ meters of snow annually

youtube_data.search_query: Wasatch Citizens Series cross country ski race Soldier Hollow

videos.title: Wasatch Citizens' #4 Nordic Race Soldier Hollow 2020
videos.description: Soldier Hollow hosted the Wasatch Citizens' Race Series #4 using the steep courses prepared for the collegiate University of Utah RMISA Invitational and the Intermountain Super Junior National Qualifier races. From Olympians to novices, the infamous Hermod's Hill and Whale's Tail Descent challenged the agility of these citizen cross country ski racers. Legend says, to skate-ski Soldier Hollow, all you need is V1 and tuck!!
videos.curation_reason: Recent coverage of the challenging Soldier Hollow course featuring the infamous Hermod's Hill and Whale's Tail Descent with detailed terrain description.

videos.title: Wasatch Citizen's Soldier Hollow Skate09
videos.description: An exciting skate race at Soldier Hollow for the Wasatch Citizen's Series #4, TUNA, SLNST, and The Utah Winter Games. February 21, 2009.

videos.title: Soldier Hollow, Women's Nordic Classic 10km
videos.description: Watch the long version of the women racing the tough 10km course at Soldier Hollow. This was a very complicated race as it was the University of Utah Invitational, RIMISA, collegiate race and the Intermountain Junior Super National Qualifier inviting all western Junior Divisions - nearly 700 racers. Follow the lead pack of skiers with Sydney Palmer-Leger (17) of Park City (blue Sun Valley uniform) and a pack of current University of Utah skiers along with Novie McCabe (18) (signed with Univ. Utah for next year along with Sydney Palmer-Leger). The Utah Nordic Alliance (TUNA) was represented well with Tory Peters (2X Junior Nationals), Allison Oler, and Victoria Eggert. Joining the Intermountain Division in this largest of US Western Junior races were Pacific Northwest, Far West, High Plains, and Rocky Mountain Divisions.

videos.title: Wasatch Citizens' Series #5 Soldier Hollow 17km, 2015
videos.description: The Soldier Hollow ribbon of snow skied well for the Wasatch Citizens' grand finale. The sky was a mix of snow, low clouds, and sunny moments. With the American Birkie and the McCall JNQ and general interest in bicycling weather, the turnout for the race was limited. Big mistake to miss this race. The course was designed for national level competitors giving the citizens a bit more of a workout. It was good for us. The course began with a long climb up the hollow to top out on Roller Coaster with a few sharp corners unto Going Home and Big Buckaroo, onto the Sprint loop dropping into the stadium to do it again, four times.
videos.curation_reason: Shows the challenging national-level course design with varied weather conditions and detailed course description including Roller Coaster section.

videos.title: Wasatch Citizens' Series #4, Sit-Skiers and Adult Stand-up 2017
videos.description: Last week's cold temperatures and hard snow are swirling their way down the river. Soldier Hollow provided a XC-cross experience for these citizen racer  - sadly off camera. The conversations were not "did you fall?" but "how many times did you go down?"  The surviving tracks were left from the snow-making and hard grooming of Junior worlds so these citizen racers took on the much harder than usual, world-competition-level, racing course. Look on...

videos.title: Soldier Hollow, Men's Nordic Classic Race
videos.description: Watch three hill climbing and hill bombing laps for a 10km Classic race at Soldier Hollow, Utah. Several races were staged together to be separated out on paper later, but on the snow, the University of Utah western Invitational (RMISA) collegiate skiers raced along with the Intermountain Division invitational to all the western states' Junior National Qualifiers. Western Junior divisions included Pacific Northwest, Far West, High Plains, Intermountain, and Rocky Mountain. Go TUNA skiers (The Utah Nordic Alliance).

videos.title: Wasatch Citizens' Series #4 Juniors, Soldier Hollow 2017
videos.description: Right out of the 2002 Olympic stadium and up under the bridge came the Junior skiers of northern Utah. Warm temperatures and slushy conditions may have slowed the skis but not the enthusiasm of these skiers.

videos.title: Wasatch Citizen's Classic Nordic Race Dec. 08
videos.description: Soldier Hollow was the venue for the opening Wasatch Citizen's Race on December 20th, 2008.  The Utah Nordic Alliance (TUNA), Salt Lake Nordic Ski Team, Park City Ski Team, Soldier Hollow Ski Team, and the Univesrity of Utah ski team joined the citizen's race for over 160 skiers in 10 waves.

videos.title: Wasatch Citizens' Race #2 Jr 2013
videos.description: White Pine at Park City hosted race #2 under a perfectly blue sky while US Nationals paused between race days. Skiers from out-of-state came over from Nationals to race in this Wasatch Citizens' race mixing it up with the local kids.

videos.title: Wasatch Citizens' Series Race #1, Dec. 2015-16
videos.description: Welcome back to the best citizens' race series! Watch another perfect Utah day of sunshine, cold temperatures, and fast skiing at Soldier Hollow, Utah. Snow is piling up in Utah allowing  the great folks a Soldier Hollow to groom out both their artificial snow base and natural snow into solid classic tracks. The University of Utah skiers dominated the race front of the race, along with returning Utah college skiers back for the holiday. Great to have so many fast skiers in every age group.

videos.title: Wasatch Citizens' Series Race #1, Classic
videos.description:
White Pine of Park City hosted the first Wasatch Citizens' Race of the Year, a classic race. 
What is classic racing? Right now it is only defined by what it is not. It is skiing but not skating during a race. 
The concern with classic skiing is that top skiers are winning classic races without kick wax and on single camber skis by double poling the entire race on glide wax only. The kick and glide is gone. Those of us who were skiing in the 70’s and 80’s get this déjà vu sense. Classic skiing has been in trouble before. Do we need a third category of racing? Classic, Freestyle, and Double-Pole-without-skating? Probably won’t go that route. FIS instituted an 83% rule this year. To reduce your double pole effectiveness, our classic ski poles can be no longer than 83% of your height standing in ski boots – because who wants to be standing outdoors in your socks in the line before a race? Since the biggest advantage of double poling on glide-wax-only is on the flats and descents, perhaps a majority of future classic races will be point to point, always rising. 
Not everyone agrees with double poling a whole classic race, finding it compelling that classic skiing isn’t classic without kick and glide. Somehow, usually by using hills too steep and too long to be advantaged by double poling, the FIS is hoping to get the top skiers back onto classic skis with kick wax. Another opinion, expressed by age group winner and possibly the only age group winner to double pole this race, Tyler Cruickshank, “…there is an added beauty, cunning, and legitimacy for each racer to contemplate each classic race course and their own fitness and ability to double pole or to kick and glide on that day.”
